reasoning: RFC 8785 §3.1 defines JCS canonical form; an external official spec (location_hash anchored, provenance_strength=strong) is sufficient anchor evidence per AEP v0.5 axiom 2.
text: AEP v0.5 strict canonical JSON profile is defined as RFC 8785 (JCS) extended with: rejection of duplicate keys, rejection of NaN/Infinity, numeric canonicalization through arbitrary-precision Decimal AST, and hashing of re-serialized strict AST rather than raw permissive parse.

reasoning: Per AEP v0.5.5 §V55-CONSOLIDATION, cross-runtime (Python/Node.js/Go/Rust) numeric conformance is staged for v0.6. The 35-vector AEP-NUMERIC-v1 test corpus at test_vectors/v0_5/A.10-numeric-canonicalization/ is the conformance baseline; independent implementations target byte-identical canonical bytes.
text: Cross-runtime numeric canonicalization byte-identical agreement is unverified for AEP v0.5.5 at publication; it requires independent implementations producing identical canonical bytes against the AEP-NUMERIC-v1 conformance corpus.
